channel.title: 2026-06-20 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-06-20 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: 结合遗传算法与深度学习发现用于甲烷纯化的二维假想共价有机框架
item.description:
📚 ScienceDirect
该工作面向甲烷纯化应用，探索二维假想共价有机框架材料的计算发现。研究标题表明其结合遗传算法与深度学习进行材料筛选或结构设计，具体候选材料、筛选指标和分离性能需查阅原文。

item.title: Biomimetic ferroelectric-semiconductor transistor enables neuronal multisensory integration
item.description:
📚 Nature Communications
👤 Shuo Liu, Ligong Zhang, Ruiqing Xie, Zhiyuan Wu, Aojie Chen, Lixia Han 等19位作者
Nature Communications, Published online: 20 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74648-y Replicating the brain’s multisensory integration is vital for artificial intelligences. Liu et al. have developed biomimetic ferroelectric-semiconductor field-effect transistors to mimick the brain’s audiovisual integration driven by hardware-native multi-physics coupling.

item.title: Axion electrodynamics in a topologically trivial antiferromagnet
item.description:
📚 Nature Communications
👤 Abhilash Mishra, Robin Karothiya, Syed Qamar Abbas Shah, Arti Kashyap, Christian Binek
Nature Communications, Published online: 20 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74634-4 It is possible to realize axion electrodynamics in a material with an isotropic and linear magnetoelectric response, however, such a material has thus far evaded experimental realization. Here, Mishra et al succeed in creating such a linear isotropic magnetoelectric response in powdered Chromia.

item.title: Enthalpy‐Driven Topological Programming of (TPMS)‐Like Carbon Networks
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Jiacheng Ma, Zhengwang Liu, Pengyuan Zhu, Miao Ma, Bokun Wang, Peiyu Cui, Boyuan Zhang, Long Qin, Yifan Kang, Zhanyou Ji, Kaiping Tian, Fan Wu, Guiqiang Fei, Renchao Che, Wenhuan Huang
Advanced Materials, EarlyView.

item.title: Global utility-scale photovoltaics are structurally displaced from electricity demand centres
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Mingquan Wu, Boxiong Su, Jie Liu, Feng Yan, Zheng Niu, Fang Chen 等10位作者
The system value of solar photovoltaics (PV) increasingly depends on effectively connecting generation to electricity demand. Here, we develop an integrated framework to diagnose this spatial constraint globally. Using Sentinel-2 imagery and deep learning, we map near-global utility-scale PV plants at 10-m resolution for 2025. Coupling this with generation models and a 1-km nighttime-light-derived electricity demand surface, we estimate ~ 1,852 TWh of annual PV generation against ~ 32.8 PWh of demand, yielding a ~ 5.65% mean penetration. Structural under-coverage is widespread: ~82% and 91% of global electricity demand lie in provinces below the 8.6% and 15.7% IEA reference thresholds, respectively. Under IEA-aligned scenarios requiring ~ 3,990 and ~ 5,910 TWh of PV supply, meeting demand…

item.title: Tuning Intermediate Adsorption and Interfacial Water Networks via Lattice Strain of NiFe Alloys Boosts Low‐Concentration Nitrate Electroreduction
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Heng Yue, Wan Xie, Da Wan, Huizhu Cai, Xue Zhang, Qi Hu, Hengpan Yang, Chuanxin He
Advanced Materials, EarlyView.

item.title: A new way to control tiny quantum light sources by twisting atomically thin layers of hexagonal boron nitride
item.description:
📚 Physics News
In a paper published in Science Advances, researchers at the University of Technology Sydney (UTS) in collaboration with the University of Minnesota and Kyung Hee University have found a new way to control quantum light sources, which is one of the key elements needed before quantum technologies can be used reliably in real-world systems.

item.title: Energy-Exchange Driven Effective Field Framework (EE-DEFF): A Reproducible Variational Stochastic Model of Emergent Structures
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Md Azahar Ali
We propose a variational stochastic effective field framework describing emergent structures in non-equilibrium energy-exchange systems. The model introduces a continuous scalar field Φ(x,t) governed by structural relaxation, energy-gradient coupling, and stochastic fluctuations. Grounded in the fundamental premise that physical configuration states manifest through localized absorption and emission dynamics, we demonstrate that stable particle-like structures correspond to metastable extrema of an effective action functional. Incorporating numerical validation under explicit bounds, the proposed framework shows significantly enhanced convergence stability and scaling performance over classical stochastic baselines. In the diffusion limit, the model maintains mathematical consistency with…

item.title: Responses of the macroalgae Ulva lactuca (Ulvales, Chlorophyta) and Sargassum cymosum (Fucales, Ochrophyta) to thermal stress evaluated by PAM fluorometry
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Willian de Souza Costa, Vinicius Peruzzi de Oliveira, Heitor Monteiro Duarte, Carlos Alberto de Moura Barboza, Lisia Monica de Souza Gestinari
There is growing concern regarding the thermal effects of global warming on benthic marine macroalgae, which are ecologically important ecosystem engineers on rocky shores. Metabolic responses to thermal stress vary among species and depend on their optimal temperature and tolerance range. Physiological studies using pulse amplitude modulated (PAM) fluorometry to assess photosystem II are essential for understanding macroalgal responses to temperature fluctuations. This study evaluated photosystem II response behavior under extreme temperature conditions in Ulva lactuca Linnaeus and Sargassum cymosum C. Agardh to detect the sensitivity of these species to temperature variation. Maximum and effective quantum efficiencies (Fv/Fm and ΔF/Fm′) and photosystem II thermal tolerance were measured…

item.title: Synergistic Experimental and Quantum Chemical Investigation of Heavy Metal Immobilization in Industrial Soils
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Manuraj S, Mithun karthick K, Sridhar K
Industrial discharge from the textile and dyeing industries in Tamil Nadu’s Tiruppur district has severely contaminated the soil with heavy metals, posing significant ecological and human health risks. This study assesses vermicomposting as a sustainable and eco-friendly bioremediation strategy for detoxifying these heavy metal-contaminated soils. Industrially polluted soil samples were subjected to vermicomposting using Eisenia fetida with organic amendments such as cattle dung and decomposed plant residues under controlled conditions for 45–60 days. Changes in soil properties and heavy metal concentrations were measured before and after treatment using Atomic Absorption Spectrophotometry (AAS). The results showed a substantial reduction in all analysed heavy metals with cadmium removal…

item.title: Microemulsion-Assisted Formation of P3P6T: PCBM Bulk Heterojunctions via Ultrasonic Spray Deposition for application in Organic Solar Cells
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Mahendra B. Patil, Hemant S. Tarkas, Shubham S. Patil, Sandesh R. Jadkar, Jaydeep V. Sali
Aqueous-processable conjugated polymers offer an environment-friendly pathway for organic solar cell fabrication. In this work, a bulk-heterojunction(BHJ) comprising water soluble poly(3-(potassium-6-hexanoate) thiophene-2,5-diyl) (P3P6T) as donor and(6,6(-phenyl-C-61-butyric acid methyl ester)) (PCBM) as acceptor was synthesized using a microemulsion approach in combination with ultrasonic spray deposition. Two orthogonal solvents, water for P3P6T and dichlorobenzene for PCBM were used. These were mixed with isopropanol in specific proportions, to form a stable microemulsion. Use of orthogonal solvents for the donor and acceptor enabled controlled separation of donor–acceptor pure phases during spray deposition. Morphological and optical studies revealed that as-deposited films exhibited…
